title: Reign in Spain: Patterson's Catalan Coronation
title_candidates: Reign in Spain: Patterson's Catalan Coronation
theme: Patterson wins his first Trophy at a canter, but only after surviving the tournament's most spectacular blow-up of his own making — the cataclysm at the 4th in R1 — and waiting for Gregg Williams, who led for two days, to spontaneously combust on the Stadium course.
tone: house — faithful, tongue-in-cheek, Ronay/Peck-flavoured; insider audience
opening_hook: Open on the 4th green of the Stadium course in Round 3: Gregg Williams walking off with a 9, John Patterson walking off with the outright Trophy lead he will never relinquish — then flash back to Tuesday on the Tour course and the small matter of Patterson's own 12.
foreshadow: Patterson's 12 at the Tour 4th in R1 — planted early as the round he should have lost the tournament, paid off when he wins it by 10 anyway.
Gregg Williams's personal-best gross +12 on day one — planted as the form line, paid off when the same player makes 11 at the Stadium 13th.
Jon Baker quietly lurking at the top of the Gross board from hole one — planted as a low-key opener, paid off with a Green Jacket by 8.
David Mullin not bottom of anything for three rounds — paid off with a Sunday-morning capitulation at the 3rd.

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Patterson trailed Gregg Williams by a single point after each of the first two rounds on the Tour course, despite shipping a 12 at the 4th in R1 and a 10 at the 15th in R2. The tournament turned on the front nine of the Stadium course in R3: Williams briefly snatched outright lead at the 3rd (b21), Patterson took it back at the 4th when Williams made 9 (b18/b15), and any thought of a contest ended at the 13th when Williams made 11 (b05). Patterson then piled up 19 points across holes 12–17 (b10) to lead by 8, and a serene 40 on Sunday widened it to 10.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Williams set the early pace with a personal-best +12 on day one (b07) and led by 5, then by 1 after R2. Baker, who had sat first or joint-first from hole one of the tournament, took outright control at the Stadium 4th in R3 (b17) when Williams's wheels came off; from there a +18 / +17 weekend on the Stadium turned a 1-shot deficit into an 8-shot win at +63 — the third-best Jacket score in TEG history (b04). Williams's consolation was his own personal-best Gross total of +76 (b11).

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: For three rounds the Spoon belonged to Alex Baker, who finished bottom of every individual round. Mullin, comfortably mid-pack and in fact runner-up for the Green Jacket, arrived on Sunday in 5th. He then carded a 9 at the par-5 3rd (b27) and an 8 at the 4th (b28), dropping to last on the cumulative Stableford at R4 H3 (b19) and staying there. A late hot stretch of 14 points across 11–14 (b22) was too little, too late.

headline_candidates: Williams Sets the Tone, Patterson Sets Fire to the 4th
A Personal Best, a 12, and a One-Shot Lead
Tour de Farce: Catalunya Opens for Business
rounds.chosen_headline: Williams Sets the Tone, Patterson Sets Fire to the 4th
rounds.angle: Gregg Williams's career-best gross +12 puts him top of both boards, but the story of the day is Patterson making 12 at a par 4 and still being one off the lead — with Neumann posting a 10 and a 12 of his own for company.

headline_candidates: Status Quo on the Tour: Williams by One, Again
rounds.chosen_headline: Status Quo on the Tour: Williams by One, Again
rounds.angle: A second lap of the Tour course produces almost no movement — Williams still leads the Trophy by one and the Jacket by one — but Patterson's 10 at the 15th and Williams's own 8 at the 4th hint that the Stadium will not be kind.

headline_candidates: Stadium Rock: Patterson Takes Over, Williams Falls Apart
An 11 at the 13th and the Trophy is Settled
Catalan Coup: One Round, Two Trophies Decided
rounds.chosen_headline: Stadium Rock: Patterson Takes Over, Williams Falls Apart
rounds.angle: The pivotal round. Lead changes on the front nine give way to Williams's 9 at the 4th and 11 at the 13th; Patterson's six-hole burst from the 12th puts the Trophy lead at 8, while Baker quietly assumes outright control of the Jacket at the same hole that swung the Trophy.

headline_candidates: Coronation on the Stadium, Capitulation at the 3rd
Patterson Strolls, Mullin Slumps, Neumann Eagles
The Spoon Changes Hands at the Last Possible Moment
rounds.chosen_headline: Coronation on the Stadium, Capitulation at the 3rd
rounds.angle: Patterson cruises home for a 10-point win, Baker seals the Jacket by 8, Mullin's 9-8 start at the 3rd and 4th hands him the Spoon at the death, and Stuart Neumann lands the tournament's only eagle at the 15th.

players.arc: Survives his own 12 at the Tour 4th, lurks one point back through 36 holes, then detonates the tournament with a Stadium-course Saturday and strolls to a 10-point win — his first Trophy and the 4th-biggest Stableford total in TEG history.
Leads for two rounds with a personal-best gross +12, hangs his hat on a PB +76 total, but the Stadium course extracts a 9 and an 11 on Saturday and the Trophy with it; runner-up in both main events.
Top of the Gross board from hole one to hole 72 — a slow, patient strangulation that yields the Green Jacket by 8 and the third-best Jacket score on record.
Quietly competitive enough to be Jacket runner-up, then undone by a Sunday-morning 9-8 at the 3rd and 4th that hands him the Wooden Spoon with three holes still to go on the front nine.
A walking par-worst exhibit on the Tour course (12 at the 15th, 10 at the 13th, 8 at the 1st), redeemed on the final day by the tournament's only eagle at the Stadium 15th.
Bottom of every individual round for three days and seemingly the inevitable Spoon recipient — until Mullin produced something even worse on Sunday morning.

venue_notes: TEG's 3rd visit to Catalonia and to Camiral/PGA Catalunya: two warm-up days on the Tour course (round 3 and 4 at this venue) followed by the main event on the Stadium (rounds 4 and 5 here). Lean on the Tour-as-undercard / Stadium-as-main-stage contrast — the tournament's three competitions all flipped or were settled on the Stadium, particularly its brutal front-nine par 5s (the 3rd) and the back-nine 13th.
